description: Formalized risk assessment schema based on the WWDA DeFi protocol rubric.

subcriteria.label: #1.1 Audit Completeness Scale
subcriteria.research_instructions: Go to protocol documentation and search for 'audit', 'security', 'smart contract audit'. Find and read audit reports/PDFs. Subjectively estimate what percentage of deployed smart contracts are covered by published audits. If there is no information in documentation, search the web ("<protocol> audit", "<protocol> security review"). If no reliable audit info is found anywhere, choose 'no_audit'.

subcriteria.label: #1.2 Auditor's Reputation

scale.description: Auditor moderately known with limited history
Auditor highly regarded with positive community feedback
Auditor is top-tier firm (on WWDA 'Auditor Sheet')

subcriteria.research_instructions: Once auditors are identified from audit reports or docs, compare against WWDA's internal trusted-auditors list. If missing, research firms via web, looking at their portfolio, reputation, years in business, and community feedback. Classify according to the scale. If no audit exists, choose 'no_audit'.

subcriteria.label: #1.3 Vulnerability Resolution Scale

scale.description: No vulnerabilities or all resolved and verified

subcriteria.research_instructions: From audit reports, extract vulnerability severities and check resolution status (fixed / acknowledged / won't fix). Confirm via follow-up audits, GitHub issues, or public post-mortems if available. Map the remaining unresolved issues to the appropriate severity bucket and choose a score. If no audit exists, pick 'no_audit'.

subcriteria.label: #2.1 Hack Timing
subcriteria.research_instructions: Search for hacks/exploits involving the protocol using web search ("<protocol> hack", "<protocol> exploit", "post-mortem"). Use reputable sources such as project blog posts, security writeups, and news sites. Identify the latest significant security incident and its date. Map elapsed time since that incident to the scale. If no credible incidents found, choose 'no_hack'.

subcriteria.label: #2.2 Protocol Security Direct Breach
subcriteria.research_instructions: From the same hack/exploit research, classify the severity and number of incidents. Consider whether vulnerabilities were directly in the protocol smart contracts or in dependencies. Choose the appropriate bucket based on number and severity of discovered issues.

subcriteria.label: #2.3 Financial Losses from External Protocols

scale.description: Significant loss of all funds (own and users')
Significant loss of protocol's own funds

subcriteria.research_instructions: Investigate financial impact of past incidents, distinguishing between user losses vs protocol treasury losses and severity. Use post-mortems, incident reports, and credible news sources. Estimate how much of TVL/treasury was affected.

subcriteria.label: #2.4 Funds Recovery Post-Hack

scale.description: All funds recovered or no exploit at all

subcriteria.research_instructions: For protocols with hacks, determine what percentage of funds were ultimately recovered (via refunds, whitehat returns, treasury compensation, etc.). If no exploit occurred, use 'all_recovered_or_no_exploit'.

criteria.label: #3 Bug Bounty Program - Public Audit
criteria.research_instructions: Search protocol documentation for bug bounty; check security sections. Then search on Immunefi (https://immunefi.com/bug-bounty/) and the general web for '<protocol> bug bounty'. Determine maximum bounty size in USD and map to the scale. If no program is found, select 'no_bounty'.

criteria.label: #4 Oracle's Dependency on Third Parties
criteria.research_instructions: Inspect protocol docs for 'oracle', 'price feed', 'Chainlink', etc. If necessary, look at smart contracts on explorers to identify oracle contracts and price sources. Determine whether the protocol relies on none, one, or multiple oracles and whether they are reputable third parties (e.g. Chainlink) or custom/unclear mechanisms.

criteria.label: #5 Bridge Asset Exposure
criteria.research_instructions: Check docs and UI for use of cross-chain bridges or wrapped assets (e.g. Ethereum to Arbitrum, Optimism to Base, Bitcoin to Ethereum). If any bridging is integral to the protocol, mark 'uses_bridge'; otherwise 'no_bridge'.

criteria.label: #6 Liquidation Risk
criteria.research_instructions: Look for lending/borrowing mechanics, leveraged strategies, or collateralized positions. If deposits can be liquidated (e.g., borrow against collateral, leveraged looping), set 'has_liquidation_risk'; otherwise 'no_liquidation_risk'.

criteria.label: #7 Bad Debt Risk

scale.description: No liquidation mechanism; doesn't use third party for liquidations
No liquidation mechanism; uses third party for liquidations
Liquidation mechanism exists but bad debt exists
Liquidation mechanism exists and no bad debt observed

criteria.research_instructions: From docs and external analysis, determine whether there is a liquidation mechanism and whether bad debt is known (e.g. undercollateralized positions that were not covered). Also check if third-party liquidators are used. This criterion is subjective; document assumptions when scoring.

criteria.label: #8 Locked Assets (All Pools)
criteria.research_instructions: Retrieve TVL directly from DefiLlama or other reputable TVL metric sources. Use current TVL in USD, select the closest bucket according to the thresholds.

criteria.label: #9 Instant Withdrawal
criteria.research_instructions: From protocol docs and UI (e.g., 'Withdraw', 'Redeem' flows), determine typical withdrawal times and any lock-up periods for user deposits. Use the longest standard lock period when multiple options exist.

criteria.label: #10 Deposit Reduction
criteria.research_instructions: Review risk sections and mechanics in docs to see whether user principal can decrease (e.g., due to impermanent loss, strategy losses, slashing, or other risks). If clear risk of principal loss, select 'deposit_can_decrease'; otherwise 'deposit_stable'.

subcriteria.label: #11.1 Reward Composition

scale.description: Mainly extra tokens, some principal growth
Balanced extra tokens and principal growth
Mainly principal growth, some extra tokens

subcriteria.research_instructions: Study APR/APY explanation in docs and UI. Determine whether returns are primarily from growth in underlying assets or from distribution of additional tokens/points. This is subjective; document assumptions in reasoning.

subcriteria.label: #11.2 Reward Volatility (Market Dynamics)
subcriteria.research_instructions: For reward tokens, check price history (e.g. via CoinGecko/CoinMarketCap) over the last month. Estimate monthly volatility and map to the scale. If there is no token or rewards are not market-exposed, use 'negligible'.

subcriteria.label: #11.3 Reward Accessibility

scale.description: Immediate availability or no extra rewards

subcriteria.research_instructions: From docs and UI, determine unlock schedules for rewards (tokens/points). Note any lock-up or vesting and classify into time buckets. If rewards are available immediately or there are no extra rewards, choose 'immediate_or_no_rewards'.

criteria.label: #12 Third Parties Usage

scale.description: Yes, uses other protocols for reinvestment
No, does not reinvest into third parties

criteria.research_instructions: Study strategies/mechanics in docs and UI to see if funds are reinvested into other DeFi protocols (e.g., yield aggregators, lending loops on other platforms). If there is any counterparty protocol exposure, mark 'uses_third_parties'; otherwise 'no_third_parties'.

criteria.label: #13 Protocol Maturity
criteria.research_instructions: Estimate age from protocol docs, creation date of primary GitHub repository, and Twitter account creation date (use the oldest). Optionally cross-check with Certik Skynet 'Project Maturity Metrics' if listed. Map age to scale.

criteria.label: #14 Protocol Documentation (or Whitepaper)
criteria.research_instructions: Assess quality and completeness of docs: clarity of mechanics, deposit/withdraw flows, risk disclosures, and architecture descriptions. This is subjective—if everything is clearly explained and thorough, choose 'well_documented'.

criteria.label: #15 Locate Contract Address

scale.description: Some but not all contracts documented
All relevant contracts documented or linked

criteria.research_instructions: Check documentation for smart contract addresses or links to registry repositories (e.g., GitHub JSON lists). Evaluate how complete the list is for main contracts users might need if the UI is unavailable.

criteria.label: #16 Contract Method Explained

scale.description: All contracts and relevant methods documented

criteria.research_instructions: Look for function/method documentation for important contracts (deposit, withdraw, claim, view functions). Better projects provide ABI-level explanations for interacting via block explorers or scripts.

subcriteria.label: #17.1 Followers
subcriteria.research_instructions: Find official Twitter/X account from docs/site. Read follower count and compute followers/1000; cap at 1.0.

subcriteria.label: #17.2 Posts & Reports (Last 30 days)
subcriteria.research_instructions: Manually or via API count posts in last 30 days. If <5, score 0. If 5–10, use posts/10. If ≥10, score 1.

subcriteria.label: #17.3 Twitter account age
subcriteria.research_instructions: Use account creation date and compare to current date; map to age buckets.

criteria.label: #18 Discord (Activity and Popularity)

scale.description: Active every day for last 3 days

criteria.research_instructions: Join official Discord (link from docs/site/Twitter). Inspect general/dev channels for activity and responses from team over last 3 days; classify according to scale.

subcriteria.label: #19.1 Followers
subcriteria.research_instructions: From main GitHub org or repo, read followers and compute followers/1000; cap at 1.0.

subcriteria.label: #19.2 Stars
subcriteria.research_instructions: Aggregate stars across core repos; compute stars/1000; cap at 1.0.

subcriteria.label: #19.3 Forks
subcriteria.research_instructions: Aggregate forks across core repos; compute forks/1000; cap at 1.0.

subcriteria.label: #19.4 Commits
subcriteria.research_instructions: Use 'Github Commits Formula' from external sheet (https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1WytQnlrPtfYQFc8MzgoTHBezOx4bchbfZAraJ_Nlg6M/edit?gid=1204815281). Apply that formula to commit history across repos.

criteria.label: #20 Other Social Activity (Telegram, Medium, Mirror, Reddit, etc.)

scale.description: Active Telegram (admin message in last 3-7 days)

criteria.research_instructions: Find additional social channels from docs/site/Twitter (Telegram, Medium, Mirror, Reddit, etc.). Check recent activity; if any channel is actively maintained per criteria, assign score 1. If none, score 0.

criteria.label: #21 Contact Developers

scale.description: No clear way to contact team

criteria.research_instructions: Look for support channels such as Discord tickets, Intercom/Zendesk, contact forms, emails, or direct contact via social networks. If any official support/contact method is obvious and working, set 'contact_possible'; otherwise 'contact_not_possible'.

criteria.label: #22 Top Tier Funds, Angels, Investors
criteria.research_instructions: Use https://cryptorank.io/ to find project funding rounds and investors. If missing, search web for '<protocol> funding round'. Then match investor categories against WWDA's investor tier mapping and select score. If no investment info is available, score 0.

criteria.label: #23 WWDA Deposit History
criteria.research_instructions: This depends on internal WWDA tables of previous interactions with the protocol. For external tools, this may always be 0 unless integrated with WWDA's own data store.

criteria.label: #24 Defi Safety
criteria.research_instructions: Look up protocol on https://defisafety.com/app. If absent, score 0. If present, read score and map to thresholds. Optionally, also consider additional third-party ratings (e.g., Certik Skynet at https://skynet.certik.com/) as future extensions.
